operating_state: Total revenues increased 81% to $18.76 billion driven by 122,220 additional vehicle deliveries year-over-year, primarily Model 3 and Model Y units. Production ramping at Gigafactory Shanghai and higher Model Y mix drove favorable sales composition despite supply chain constraints. Energy generation and storage revenue grew from increased Powerwall and Megapack deployments, partially offset by solar component import delays. Manufacturing operations depend on limited battery cell suppliers including Panasonic and CATL, with plans to supplement through internal cell production.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded 780 basis points to 29.1% with automotive gross margin reaching 32.9% versus 26.5% prior year. Margin improvement driven by localized procurement and manufacturing cost reductions in China, favorable Model Y production mix, and increased regulatory credit sales. Energy segment gross margin improved through higher-margin Powerwall deployments and Solar Roof cost reductions. Cost structure remains vulnerable to raw material price volatility for lithium, nickel, and cobalt used in battery production.
material_events: Gigafactory Berlin commenced Model Y production in March 2022 with first deliveries from Gigafactory Texas beginning April 2022 incorporating 4680 in-house battery cells. Tesla announced plans to request stockholder approval for increasing authorized common shares to enable a stock split via stock dividend. The company relocated its principal executive offices to Austin, Texas during the quarter.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 Shanghai lockdowns temporarily shut Gigafactory Shanghai and supply chain components in early 2022. Global semiconductor shortages continue requiring manufacturing workarounds and alternative parts programming. Rising raw material, commodity, logistics and labor costs from pandemic-related supply chain disruptions and inflation pressures contributed to product pricing adjustments. The company faces exposure to foreign currency fluctuations affecting both revenues and costs.
forward_outlook: Capital expenditures projected at $5.00 to $7.00 billion annually supporting global factory ramps and battery cell manufacturing development. Management expects continued self-funding capability through operating cash flow generation exceeding capital spend levels. Production growth dependent on successful ramp execution at Gigafactory Berlin and Texas facilities plus expanding battery cell supply through proprietary manufacturing capabilities.
uncertainty_profile: Manufacturing ramp contingencies at new international facilities may be exacerbated by concurrent project execution, component constraints, and labor shortages. Digital asset holdings of $1.96 billion fair value subject to impairment charges on market declines without corresponding upward revaluation until sale. Autopilot and FSD features face evolving regulatory frameworks that may restrict functionality or delay deployment.
